Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, proteinuria, glycosuria, ketonuria, and low urine specific gravity, suggesting impaired renal function with possible diabetic nephropathy or acute kidney injury. Cloudy, brown urine with ammoniacal odor may indicate underlying urinary tract pathology. Further clinical correlation and nephrology evaluation are recommended.

coding.display: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus

Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
